questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.hint: Add place by place from right to left, and don't forget to carry your tens!

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.hint: Divide each digit by 3, starting from the thousands place.
questions.explanation: 3 goes into 3 one time. 3 goes into 1 zero times. Bring down 2 to make 12. 3 goes into 12 four times. 3 goes into 3 one time. The quotient is 1041.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: John reads 15 pages of his book every day. How many pages will he read in one full week (7 days)?
questions.hint: Multiply the number of pages he reads per day by the number of days in a week.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: Emma buys 3 notebooks that cost $4 each and 2 pens that cost $2 each. What is the total cost?
questions.hint: Find the cost of the notebooks and the cost of the pens separately, then add them together.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: The mixed number 1 1/4 is the same as which improper fraction?
questions.hint: Remember that 1 whole is equal to 4/4.
questions.explanation: 1 whole equals 4/4. Add the extra 1/4 to get 5/4.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: What is 1 minus 2/7? Write your answer as a fraction (e.g., a/b).
questions.hint: Change the 1 whole into a fraction with 7 as the denominator.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: A triangle with all three sides of equal length is called what?
questions.explanation: An equilateral triangle has three equal sides and three equal angles.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: What is the perimeter of a rectangle that has a length of 10 cm and a width of 4 cm?
questions.hint: Perimeter is the distance all the way around. Add length + length + width + width.

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.q: How many inches are in 3 feet? (Hint: 1 foot = 12 inches)
questions.hint: Multiply the number of feet by 12.
questions.explanation: Since 1 foot is 12 inches, 3 feet is 3 × 12 = 36 inches.
